title: Stock Market Highlights for July 7: Sensex drops 104 pts, Nifty closes at 24,399 ending 4-day gaining streak - The HinduBusinessLine
text:
Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: Markets snapped their four-session winning streak. Sensex fell 104.35 points or 0.13% to settle at 78,180.72 on Tuesday, while the Nifty 50 declined 31.65 points or 0.13% to close at 24,398.70.

Shares were ⁠largely positive through the day, consolidating gains from mostly upbeat pre-quarterly results updates and lower crude prices. The monsoon’s steady ‌progress across the country and the return of foreign inflows also aided sentiment.

Most sectoral indices ended lower, with realty and metal stocks leading the decline. However, the Nifty IT index bucked the trend and emerged as the top-performing sector. The heavyweight IT index rose 2.4%, extending its July rebound to 6.2%, as U.S. rate-hike bets remained lower than before.

Twelve of the 16 major sectors ⁠logged losses. The broader small-caps and mid-caps fell about 0.6% and 0.3%, respectively. Among other stocks, Titan ‌gained 2.7% after reporting a 41% growth in consumer business in the first quarter.

Twelve of the 16 major sectors ⁠logged losses.

The rupee ended at 94.9675 per dollar, up 0.4% from its previous close, after oscillating between modest gains and losses ‌in early trading.

CERC has allowed Tata Power arm TP Saurya to continue selling infirm solar power to third parties until partial commissioning of its hybrid project.

Markets snapped their four-session winning streak. Sensex fell 104.35 points or 0.13% to settle at 78,180.72 on Tuesday, while the Nifty 50 declined 31.65 points or 0.13% to close at 24,398.70.

Most sectoral indices ended lower, with realty and metal stocks leading the decline. However, the Nifty IT index bucked the trend and emerged as the top-performing sector.

Cult.fit has filed IPO papers with SEBI to raise ₹950 crore, aiming to expand fitness centres, invest in Cultsport and repay debt after strong FY26 growth.

Cult.fit Ltd (previously Curefit Healthcare Pvt Ltd), India’s largest fitness services provider, has filed its Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP) with SEBI for an Initial Public Offering (IPO).

Defence stocks drop over 2% as profit-taking occurs ahead of a seasonally soft Q1FY27 earnings forecast, analysts warn.

H.G. Infra Engineering: Six stepdown wholly owned subsidiaries of the company have been struck off by the ROC.

Sensex declined 98.72 pts or 0.13% to 78,186.35 at 3.16 pm, and Nifty 50 slipped by 42.90 pts or 0.18% to 24,387.45.

SBI Funds Management, India’s largest asset ‌manager, will draw investments from Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA) and Singapore’s GIC as ​part of its $1.2 billion initial public offering, two sources with direct knowledge of ⁠the matter said.

Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd are restoring their AE15 Gemini service via the Suez Canal, reducing Asia-Europe transit times amid improving Red Sea security.

Serentica Renewables on Tuesday said it has inked a power purchase agreement with Solar Energy Corporation of India for the supply of electricity from a 600MW Firm and Dispatchable Renewable Energy project (FDRE).

The certification affirms HCLTech’s readiness to support enterprises in scaling AI adoption with strong governance, risk management and operational rigor. Shares traded 3% positive at ₹1,169.50 on the NSE.

📈 Sensex: 78,387.48 ▲102.41 (+0.13%)📈 Nifty 50: 24,461.45 ▲31.10 (+0.13%)

Tata Power plans a new solar facility in Odisha, driving growth in renewable energy with over 26 GW generation capacity.

Adani's Kutch Copper unit achieves LME certification, enhancing its global credibility and positioning in the refined copper market.

SBI Mutual Fund, India’s largest asset management company, today announced the launch of a Target Maturity Index Fund - SBI CRISIL-IBX SDL Index – June 2034 Index Fund. The New Fund Offer (NFO) will be open from July 7 – 14, 2026.

The SBI CRISIL – IBX SDL Index – June 2034 Index Fund is an open-ended Target Maturity Index Fund tracking the CRISIL-IBX SDL Index – June 2034 Index. A relatively high-Interest rate risk and relatively low credit risk. The investment objective of the scheme is to provide returns that closely correspond to the total returns of the securities as represented by the underlying index, subject to tracking error. However, there is no guarantee or assurance that the investment objective of the scheme will be achieved. The scheme doesn’t assure or guarantee any returns.

The SBI CRISIL – IBX SDL Index – June 2034 Index Fund will primarily invest between 95% and 100% of its assets in securities forming part of CRISIL-IBX SDL Index - June 2034. The balance up to 5% of the assets of fund may be invested in debt and money market instruments, including cash and cash equivalents

Gold prices on Tuesday dropped by ₹1,417 to ₹1,45,500 per 10 grams in futures trade amid a fall in spot demand.

P N Gadgil shares flat at Rs 583.55 on the NSE.

The Company delivered a strong performance in Q1 FY27, repor ng robust revenue growth of 41% YoY. The Retail segment grew 56% YoY, with robust same-store sales growth (SSSG) of 46% YoY as the primary driver, indica ng that the growth was substan ally organic in nature, driven by deeper customer engagement at exis ng stores. As a result, the Retail segment contributed around 78% of the total revenue, which is meaningfully higher on a YoY basis. Franchise and E-com business grew by 8% & 20% respec vely YoY. Share of gold bullion sales in Retail revenue is normalized and on a stable level of ~ 22% as compared to a higher share in the previous quarter.

Aye Finance reports a 28% AUM rise to ₹7,329 crore in Q1 FY27, but stock declines 5% amid weaker sequential results.

Broader market breadth on BSE remained positive, with 4,158 stocks advancing against 1,516 declines and 2,444 unchanged. A notable 198 stocks hit 52-week highs against 63 at 52-week lows

Titan reported an 41% YoY growth in its overall consumer businesses, marking the third consecutive quarter where the company has sustained an accelerated revenue growth rate
